objectives: Review hand-written Rust code across 7 crates for quality, maintainability, and correctness
Check clean code principles, SOLID, DRY, naming, error handling, documentation, and Rust idioms
Cover all files modified since the last review (git status diff)
constraints: Exclude generated/ directories from review scope
Focus on validate.rs (CLI), pattern_codegen.rs (codegen), common/validate.rs (model), xml_scan.rs, cbpr.rs, fednow.rs, sepa.rs, helpers.rs, camt053_to_mt940.rs, and all test files
important_facts: PRIOR ISSUES RESOLVED: ValidateError::ValidationFailed variant now correctly separates from Parse; cli/validate.rs now imports xml_scan helpers from the canonical location instead of duplicating them
PRIOR ISSUE STILL OPEN: mod97 duplicated in iban.rs and lei.rs
PRIOR ISSUE STILL OPEN: FedNow amount logic -- resolved to validate_amount() method shared between XML and typed paths -- fully deduplicated
PRIOR ISSUE STILL OPEN: parse_year Vec allocation -- not in scope of this diff but still present
PRIOR ISSUE STILL OPEN: RuleRegistry::validate_all non-deterministic HashMap ordering
NEW: pattern_codegen.rs prior parse_char_class duplication was FIXED -- now uses read_char_or_escape() helper for both escaped and unescaped paths

NEW ISSUE: cbpr.rs check_name_required() line 468 calls both has_element() AND extract_element() redundantly -- has_element is strictly weaker, so the condition simplifies to extract_element().is_none()

NEW ISSUE: camt053_to_mt940.rs extract_balance_date() and the val_date_iso block in entry_to_mt61() each contain an identical inline DateAndDateTime2Choice match -- a shared fn extract_date_from_choice() would eliminate 10 lines of duplication

NEW ISSUE: sepa.rs validate_pacs008_typed() has a duplicated block for SEPA charset check on Dbtr/Nm and Cdtr/Nm (lines 477-503) -- structurally identical except for the tag name; extractable to a closure or helper

NEW ISSUE (BEHAVIORAL): cbpr.rs check_name_required() silently succeeds when the closing tag is missing (else branch returns without error at line 464-465) -- means a malformed element like <Dbtr><Nm>Alice</Nm (no close tag) passes the CBPR_CDTR_NM_REQUIRED check
NEW ISSUE (DESIGN): pattern_codegen.rs emit_variable_length() emits an IIFE closure for the validator body without a code comment explaining why -- this is subtle and worth documenting
CONFIRMED FIXED: cli/validate.rs no longer has ValidateError::Parse used as exit signal -- ValidationFailed { error_count } is the correct variant now
CONFIRMED FIXED: cli/validate.rs no longer duplicates extract_element/extract_all_elements -- uses xml_scan imports
roundtrip_all.rs: make_fi() helper is duplicated verbatim in pacs_009, pain_001, pain_013, camt_056, camt_029 modules -- same struct shape each time; minor test-file DRY violation
roundtrip_all.rs: fixture() helper path computation uses relative path ../../testdata; documented with a comment explaining why -- acceptable
cli_integration.rs: all tests call Command::new(bin_path()) inline -- acceptable; no shared setup needed
snapshots.rs: XSD paths via concat!(env!(...)) -- pattern is correct and idiomatic for test files
validate.rs (model/common): Validatable trait and blanket impls are clean; IsoMessage convenience method is correct
decisions_made: No code changes made - review only
Severity classification this iteration: High=2 (new behavioral bug in cbpr check_name_required, duplicate date extraction in camt053_to_mt940), Suggestions=3
Cumulative across all reviews: Critical=0, High=8, Suggestions=15
risks_issues: BEHAVIORAL BUG: cbpr.rs check_name_required() silently passes when closing tag is missing (no error reported for truncated XML) -- medium risk for production XML with malformed partial elements

BEHAVIORAL BUG: cbpr.rs check_name_required() line 468 -- redundant has_element() || extract_element().is_none() -- always equals extract_element().is_none() since has_element is weaker; logic is correct but misleading
DRY: camt053_to_mt940.rs DateAndDateTime2Choice match duplicated in extract_balance_date() and entry_to_mt61()
DRY: sepa.rs typed path -- charset check on Dbtr/Nm and Cdtr/Nm is a copy-paste block
DRY: mod97 still duplicated in iban.rs and lei.rs (prior finding, still open)
DRY: RuleRegistry::validate_all non-deterministic error order (prior finding, still open)
DRY: roundtrip_all.rs make_fi() duplicated across 5 test modules
DESIGN: pattern_codegen.rs IIFE closure in emit_variable_length() undocumented

iteration.summary: Third full-pass code quality review covering all 13 files in the git diff. Two previously flagged issues are now fixed: ValidateError::ValidationFailed is a proper dedicated variant (no longer abusing Parse), and cli/validate.rs imports xml_scan helpers from the canonical module instead of duplicating them. pattern_codegen.rs parse_char_class duplication was also resolved via read_char_or_escape(). New findings this pass: check_name_required() in cbpr.rs has a silent-pass behavioral bug when the closing tag is missing, and has a redundant double-check (has_element + extract_element) that is logically equivalent to just extract_element; camt053_to_mt940.rs duplicates the DateAndDateTime2Choice match in two functions; sepa.rs typed-path charset check on Dbtr/Nm and Cdtr/Nm is copy-paste. Test files (roundtrip_all.rs, cli_integration.rs, snapshots.rs, translation.rs) are thorough and well-structured. No critical issues.
outputs_produced: Full code review report (in assistant response)
Updated context file superseding prior review
next_actions: Fix cbpr.rs check_name_required() to emit an error when closing tag is missing (currently returns silently)
Simplify cbpr.rs check_name_required() line 468 to just extract_element(block, 'Nm').is_none()
Extract shared fn extract_date_choice(v: &DateAndDateTime2Choice) -> String in camt053_to_mt940.rs
Extract sepa.rs charset check into a closure or fn check_sepa_charset_field(nm: Option<&Max140Text>, path, errors)
Extract mod97 into crates/mx20022-validate/src/rules/checkdigit.rs and import from iban.rs and lei.rs
Add doc comment to pattern_codegen.rs emit_variable_length() explaining the IIFE closure pattern
Extract make_fi() into a test helper module shared by roundtrip_all.rs test submodules

mentions.relevance_note: cbpr.rs silent-pass on malformed/truncated XML is also a security concern -- an attacker could craft XML with a missing closing tag to bypass CBPR_DBTR_NM_REQUIRED check
